disclaimer: This dashboard is a decision-support tool based on open-source intelligence (OSINT). It is NOT official guidance. Consult your embassy and local authorities for evacuation decisions.

sections.title: Air Defense / Interceptor Effectiveness
sections.summary: Day 12: MOD stopped reporting daily intercept breakdowns — now just says 'engaged.' 7 cruise missiles deployed (vs 8 total in first 11 days). Cruise missiles are harder to intercept. Drone/ballistic volume low but weapons mix escalating.
sections.analysis: Two alarming signals: (1) Iran deployed 7 cruise missiles in one day — nearly doubling the entire war's cruise missile count. Cruise missiles fly low and fast, harder to intercept than drones or ballistic missiles. Iran's ~3,000 cruise missile stockpile has been the war's sleeping giant. (2) MOD stopped reporting daily intercept/hit breakdowns, switching to 'engaged.' This opacity could mean intercept rates are declining and they don't want to publish the numbers. No new deaths suggests today's salvos were handled, but the weapons mix escalation is significant.

sections.title: Attack Volume on UAE
sections.summary: Day 12: 6 ballistic + 39 drones + 7 cruise (52 total). Drone volume rising again (39 vs 18 two days ago). Cruise missile deployment is a major weapons mix escalation. Campaign NOT winding down despite Trump's rhetoric.
sections.analysis: Campaign is NOT de-escalating (+7.1% vs prior report). Ballistic missile plateau at 16-17/day broke the earlier decline trend. Iran's unused cruise missile stockpile (~3,000) remains the biggest wildcard. IRGC publicly stated intent to expand scale and intensity.

sections.title: Airport & Airspace Operations
sections.summary: Two drones fell near DXB today (Mar 11) — 4 injured. Air traffic 'operating as normal' per Dubai Media Office. Emirates gradually expanding. Third DXB-area incident of the war.
sections.analysis: Emirates and flydubai resumed limited schedules as of Mar 9 after temporary suspension on Mar 7. Emirates targeting 100% capacity 'in coming days.' However, multiple foreign carriers (Virgin Atlantic, flynas) have suspended Dubai routes. DXB airspace subject to intermittent closures during interception events. Muscat (MCT) remains the reliable alternative.

sections.title: Ports & Cargo Continuity
sections.summary: Hormuz traffic down 90% (MarineTraffic). CSIS confirms Iran 'largely successful' in shutting it down. No ships transited Mar 1-2. East coast ports (Khorfakkan/Fujairah) handling diverted cargo.
sections.analysis: Dubai is executing a pre-planned contingency: ships dock on the Gulf of Oman side, containers truck overland ~130-150km to Jebel Ali. Adds cost and time but keeps goods flowing. Bloomberg confirms Hormuz is 'all but closed.' East coast bypass maintains supply chain at reduced capacity.

sections.title: Retail Prices & Consumer Stress
sections.summary: UAE government actively policing price gouging. 4,468 inspections, 449 warnings, Dh176K in fines. Food supplies confirmed adequate. Shelves stocked.
sections.analysis: Economy Minister personally toured Al Awir Central Market (main wholesale hub). Government projecting normalcy and control. 420+ additional inspections planned for Ramadan. Price gouging is happening but being enforced against. No food shortages reported.

sections.title: Oman Border & Muscat Viability
sections.summary: Oman confirmed as primary evacuation hub (NYT). 600+ evacuated by bus. Oman Air running Sharjah-Muscat bus link. BA/QR/Emirates/Etihad joint British evacuation via Oman. Border ~1hr queue.

sources.name: The Traveler - Oman Air bus link

sections.analysis: Iran is deliberately NOT targeting Oman — they need the neutral corridor for diplomacy and trade. Only 8 drones total, no missiles, no recent attacks. This is the primary evacuation route: Dubai → Al Ain → Hili/Buraimi border → Muscat → fly out. Route validated by multiple evacuees.

sections.title: Civilian & Infrastructure Escalation
sections.summary: 6 dead (now includes Emirati nationals — first time). 2 new deaths today. 122 injuries. 2 armed forces martyred (reported yesterday). No confirmed deliberate civilian targeting but drone penetration rate surging.
sections.analysis: All UAE fatalities and residential damage to date are from interception debris, not deliberate targeting of civilian areas. This is consistent with IRGC targeting US military installations (Al-Dhafra, Al-Minhad) rather than cities. However, debris risk is real and random. No UAE critical infrastructure has been struck.

sections.title: Market-Implied Conflict Duration
sections.summary: Oil whiplash: spiked to $119.50 then crashed to ~$86 after Trump's 'war is very complete' comments. Markets erased steep losses. Signal: markets WANT to price in an end — any credible de-escalation will trigger a massive relief rally.

sources.name: CNBC - Oil drops on Trump signal

sections.analysis: Oil's $119→$86 crash on a single Trump comment tells you everything: markets are desperate for an off-ramp and will aggressively price in any de-escalation signal. The $119 peak represents the worst-case Hormuz scenario; the $86 landing represents 'Trump might end this soon.' Until a formal ceasefire, expect continued volatility between these bounds. Gold recovering from margin-call lows.

sections.title: Conflict Endgame — Is Tehran Still Coherent?
sections.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei officially named Supreme Leader. Trump shifted to 'war is very complete' / 'close to end' — first de-escalation signal from the US. Oil crashed 20% on the comment. But no formal ceasefire framework exists yet.

metrics.label: US Staff Ordered Out of KSA

sources.name: Investopedia - Stocks erase losses on Trump
Business Insider - Oil drops on Trump signal

sections.analysis: Two contradictory signals: (1) Trump's 'war is very complete' is the first US de-escalation rhetoric — markets responded instantly with oil crashing from $119 to $86. (2) But Iran FM still rejects ceasefire, IRGC announced it 'will decide end of war,' and Mojtaba Khamenei's appointment may harden Iran's position (he's IRGC-aligned, not a moderate). The gap between Trump declaring victory and Iran continuing to fight is the key tension. If Trump means it, a unilateral US ceasefire could emerge — but Israel may not comply. Watch for: Trump-Netanyahu call, any IRGC volume change, Mojtaba's first public statement.

red.note: Day 11: Drone intercept at 74.3% — well below 85% threshold. 9 hit UAE territory.

red.trigger: Cruise missile wave targeting UAE cities
red.note: Day 12: 7 cruise missiles in single day. Cumulative now 15 (was 8 for first 11 days).
Bahrain desal hit — UAE not yet
3 drone/debris incidents near DXB. Latest Mar 11: 2 drones, 4 injured. Ops continue.

amber.note: No confirmed deliberate targeting — all residential damage is interception debris
Resumed limited schedule Mar 9 after Mar 7 suspension

amber.trigger: Second UAE fatality from direct hit
Iran deploys cruise missiles at Gulf cities

green.note: Trump: 'war very complete' — first US de-escalation signal. No formal ceasefire yet.
